title: GitHub Review by sourcery-ai[bot]
summary: The reviewer wants two changes to `tests/ci/verify_chaos_ci_pipeline.sh`: (1) make assertions less brittle by matching individual `needs` entries instead of exact full-line/ordering matches with sed-based extraction, and (2) extract the repeated `e2e-chaos` job section parsing and common assertions into shared helper functions to reduce duplication.
external_feedback:
Hey - I've left some high level feedback:

- The new `tests/ci/verify_chaos_ci_pipeline.sh` script is very tightly coupled to the exact formatting and ordering in `ci.yaml` (e.g., full `needs:` line match, sed-based section extraction), which will make harmless workflow refactors noisy; consider making the assertions more resilient (e.g., matching individual `needs` entries or using smaller, more focused regexes) to reduce brittleness.
- Several checks in `verify_chaos_ci_pipeline.sh` manually repeat the pattern of extracting the `e2e-chaos` job section and then asserting on it; factoring that extraction and common assertions into small helper functions would simplify the script and make future changes to the job structure easier to accommodate.

<details>
<summary>Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

~~~markdown
Please address the comments from this code review:

## Overall Comments
- The new `tests/ci/verify_chaos_ci_pipeline.sh` script is very tightly coupled to the exact formatting and ordering in `ci.yaml` (e.g., full `needs:` line match, sed-based section extraction), which will make harmless workflow refactors noisy; consider making the assertions more resilient (e.g., matching individual `needs` entries or using smaller, more focused regexes) to reduce brittleness.
- Several checks in `verify_chaos_ci_pipeline.sh` manually repeat the pattern of extracting the `e2e-chaos` job section and then asserting on it; factoring that extraction and common assertions into small helper functions would simplify the script and make future changes to the job structure easier to accommodate.
~~~

</details>

***

resolution_summary: Both review comments were addressed in tests/ci/verify_chaos_ci_pipeline.sh. The brittle full-line needs assertion was replaced with an assert_needs_entry() helper that matches individual dependency entries, tolerating reordering and formatting changes. Repeated inline sed-based e2e-chaos job section extraction (previously duplicated in test_e2e_chaos_timeout, test_e2e_chaos_continue_on_error, test_e2e_chaos_kind_cluster, test_e2e_chaos_builds_keystone, and test_e2e_chaos_junit_report) was centralized into an extract_yaml_job_section() helper with a single cached E2E_CHAOS_JOB_SECTION variable reused across all tests. The extract_yaml_job_section helper was also applied to test_e2e_chaos_publish_no_dependency (which extracts sections for publish jobs like build-and-push, helm-push, merge-operator-images, github-release) since it shares the same YAML section extraction pattern.
